nodes.name: 鹱科
nodes.summary: 鹱科（Procellariidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联74种鸟类，如南极鹱、Pintado petrel、Southern giant petrel等74种鸟类。

nodes.name: CR
nodes.summary: CR表示 IUCN 极危等级，当前关联56种鸟类，如勺嘴鹬、加州神鹫、菲律宾鹰等56种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鹱形目
nodes.summary: 鹱形目（Procellariiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联115种鸟类，如加岛信天翁、短尾信天翁、Northern royal albatross等115种鸟类。

nodes.name: Galápagos Marine Reserve
nodes.summary: Galápagos Marine Reserve是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Galápagos petrel，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Galápagos petrel
nodes.summary: Galápagos petrel（Galápagos petrel，学名 Pterodroma phaeopygia），分类上属于鹱形目，并归入鹱科，在图谱中标记为极危（CR）物种，已关联1个分布地点节点。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It nests on five islands including Isabela, Santiago, Floreana, San Cristobal and Santa Cruz.]] The Galápagos petrel is endemic to the Galápagos Islands. They are mainly distributed in the Galápagos Marine Reserve area, but studies revealed that individuals frequently leave the reserve and disperse northeast towards South America and up to southwards in the period between January and February. Like other gadfly petrels, these subtropical seabirds are highly pelagic and are maladapted to the terrestrial habitats. Therefore, they are usually found far from the land and will only return to their nest to breed. The nesting colonies are distributed among five islands: Santiago, Floreana, Isabela, Santa Cruz, and San Cristobal, which are all less than apart. Multiple colonies can occur within one island; each can be as large as 200 m by 300 m and are separated by a distance that varies from 300 m to 5 km. Nowadays, the Galápagos petrels commonly nest on offshore islets because their habitats have been destroyed or displaced by anthropogenic disturbances. Their nests typically occur in highlands that are at least above the sea. Most can also be found in thickly vegetated sites where the soil is soft enough to burrow deep cavities, but some individuals also choose to nest in volcanic crevices. These ground nests make the species more vulnerable to introduced predators
